description_long: This is a vintage, early 20th-century monoplane with a fabric-covered, straight high-wing design and a single open cockpit positioned at the front of the fuselage. The wings are supported by a network of external struts and wires, and each wing features a black cross insignia, suggesting a historical military context. The fuselage is slender and tapers sharply toward the rear, with a light blue tail section and a yellow accent on the upper tail surface. The engine is mounted at the nose, exposed with visible cylinders, and drives a wooden two-blade propeller. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable. The overall construction appears to be primarily wood and fabric, with some metal components for structural support and engine parts.
description: A vintage, fabric-covered monoplane with a high straight wing, exposed nose-mounted engine, and open cockpit, featuring military insignia and a light blue tail.
description_view_0: Front view showing the exposed engine, wooden propeller, open cockpit, and the full wingspan with black cross insignia.
description_view_1: Angled side view highlighting the high-wing structure, yellow accent on the tail, and the exposed engine cylinders.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the tapering fuselage, light blue tail, and the network of supporting struts and wires beneath the wings.
description_view_3: Opposite angled side view emphasizing the open cockpit, wing structure, and the fixed landing gear with tail skid.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: High-wing, straight configuration with external struts and wires, no winglets, and minimal dihedral.
attribute_engine_type: Single nose-mounted piston engine, exposed cylinders.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Fixed taildragger landing gear with two main wheels and a tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Forward-mounted, open single-seat cockpit at the front of the fuselage.
